etymology.description: semantic 氵(氵) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 中(中) (zhōng, "middle") = water rushing around or through something -> rush
etymology.interpretation: 沖(沖) (chōng, "rush") depicts water as the primary element influencing motion or pressure, with 中(中) (zhōng, "middle") providing the sound; together they aptly convey the concept of rushing water navigating obstacles.
etymology.simplification: 沖(沖) was simplified as 冲 by replacing the water radical 氵(氵) (shuǐ, "water") with a simpler form, maintaining phonetic elements.

examples.chinese: 他冲出了门(他沖出了門)。
examples.english: He rushed out of the door.

examples.chinese: 大雨冲刷了街道(大雨沖刷了街道)。
examples.english: The heavy rain washed over the streets.

examples.chinese: 我冲到车站时(我沖到車站時)，车已经走了(車已經走了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ chōng dào chēzhàn shí, chē yǐjīng zǒu le.
examples.english: When I rushed to the station, the train had already left.
